macro_summary: Monday's session saw WTI spike 2.4% to $91.87 as Iran suspended talks with the US and threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz, sending oil-sensitive stocks lower while AI momentum held firm. The S&P 500 crept up 0.37% to 758.54 but breadth was narrow; the 10-year yield ticked up 1bp to 4.47% as the Fed's hawkish hold and inflation fears persisted. Nvidia's Computex keynote lifted AI names (NVDA +5.6%, MU +7.3%), offsetting Intel's (-5.2%) and AMD's (-1.0%) losses on Nvidia's PC chip foray.
commodities_note: WTI climbed 2.4% to $91.87 on the Hormuz escalation, while gold shed 0.6% to $4,519 as haven demand rotated to oil. The VIX rose 4.6% to 16.05, reflecting geopolitical jitters. The US dollar index was flat at 99.18.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster remains overbought: all three banks are hard-blocked on RSI >70 and extensions above 5%. DBS (+10.8% vs SMA50) led the day on volume but is now the most extended. US10Y at 4.47% is still above the 4.20% NIM-relief threshold, so the dual compression headwind persists into October. The structural call: OCBC's NIM resilience and YoY profit growth are the differentiators within a cluster that's otherwise mechanically unactionable.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired by NIM compression; tactically overbought.
singapore.key_development: UOB's RSI surged from 62 to 73 on a 2% gain while DBS's RSI hit 84 — the cluster is overbought with volume confirmation waning.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster extended its extreme divergence, with memory (MU +7.3%, SK Hynix -2.1% but still >+64% vs SMA50) and Nvidia (+5.6%) leading. Intel and AMD fell on Nvidia's PC chip entry, while CRWV surged on Vera Rubin deployment. AVGO reports in two days — the key upcoming catalyst. SOXX rose 0.6% to 571.93, outperforming SPY. The cluster remains pinned in extension: all 11 names are hard-blocked, with MU, AMD, INTC, NVDA, and SK Hynix at extreme levels. Any consolidation here would be healthy for re-entry setups.
semiconductors.key_development: Nvidia's Computex N1X/RTX Spark PC chip launch pressured Intel -5.2% and AMD -1.0%, adding competitive dimensions to the semis divergence.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverged: MSFT (+2.3%) and PLTR (+2.8%) were the relative outperformers, while AMZN (-3.4%) and GOOG (-1.1%) pulled back toward their 50-day SMAs. MSFT's NVDA PC partnership and PLTR's defense momentum drove gains, but both are extended (MSFT +14.1%, PLTR +13.2% with declining SMA50). GOOG's $80B equity raise is a structural development — it underscores the capex race but dilutes. The cluster as a whole is in a defensive posture: no entry signals, with MSFT hard-blocked and AMZN/GOOG in hold patterns.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — capex confirmed, but concentration risks and dilution weigh.
big_tech.key_development: Google's $80B equity raise (per WSJ) signals the AI capex race is far from over, adding a new funding dynamic to the cluster.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI networking switch leader — directly benefits from NVDA Spectrum-X and hyperscaler 800G deployments.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist on a pullback to the 200-day SMA near $90, or after AVGO earnings confirm networking strength.

active_risks: Iran suspends talks with US, threatens full Strait of Hormuz closure in response to Israeli actions in Lebanon — per NBC News, June 1.
US and Iran exchange fresh military strikes over the weekend, complicating the stalled ceasefire talks — per CNBC, June 1.
US-China semiconductor export controls tighten: US blocks NVDA chip exports to Chinese firms outside China; China counters with investment restrictions — per news digest.
Oil supply disruption risk elevates: WTI over $91, Goldman sees $90 Brent downside but Middle East disruption could push higher — per Reuters.
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz escalation sent WTI $6 higher, lifting energy stocks and defense names (PLTR) while pressuring consumer discretionary. Bond yields edged up on inflation fears (US10Y 4.47%). Semis held up (NVDA +5.6%) as AI demand overshadows geopolitical jitters, but Intel and AMD fell on competitive concerns. Singapore banks remained bid despite oil risk.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran's Tasnim news agency reported cessation of US-Iran message exchanges and consideration of Hormuz blockade — a sharp escalation from the prior ceasefire draft hopes (per NBC News).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new entries until the Hormuz situation stabilizes or VIX drops below 22. Selective accumulation on oversold names with real AI demand (e.g., LITE, GOOG) only after the oil shock subsides.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 21 of 26 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; Hormuz escalation adds fresh inflation risk that could delay any entry setups.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop.
